# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Java Spring Boot application. Copy the JAR file, expose port 8080, and run the application. This needs to deploy quickly for our CI/CD pipeline. We'll add health checks and monitoring in phase 2.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

# Stage 1: Use a minimal, specific base image with a non-root user
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine@sha256:bff6b1a85d42c5b8e258d80f21d30a5f03b4f4c3b0c44e8c5b0c1e5a5f1d8e2 AS base

# Set labels for image metadata
LABEL maintainer="devops-team" \
      description="Spring Boot Application" \
      version="1.0"

# Create a non-root user and group with specific UID/GID
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S appgroup && \
    adduser -u 1001 -S appuser -G appgroup -h /app -s /sbin/nologin

# Stage 2: Build the final image
FROM base AS final

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Set environment variables securely (no secrets here - secrets should be injected at runtime)
ENV JAVA_OPTS="-XX:+UseContainerSupport \
               -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0 \
               -Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom \
               -Dserver.port=8080 \
               -Dmanagement.server.port=8081" \
    SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE="production"

# Copy the JAR file with specific ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup target/*.jar app.jar

# Ensure the JAR file has read-only permissions for the app user
RUN chmod 444 /app/app.jar && \
    # Remove unnecessary system packages to reduce attack surface
    apk --no-cache upgrade && \
    # Remove package manager cache
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

# Create a tmp directory owned by appuser (Spring Boot may need it)
RUN mkdir -p /app/tmp && chown appuser:appgroup /app/tmp && chmod 700 /app/tmp

# Drop all capabilities and set read-only where possible
# Expose only the application port (not management port externally by default)
EXPOSE 8080

# Set security-related filesystem permissions
RUN chmod 555 /app

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser:appgroup

# Set a read-only temp directory
ENV TMPDIR=/app/tmp

# Use exec form to avoid shell injection and ensure proper signal handling
# Do not pass secrets via command line arguments
ENTRYPOINT ["java"]
CMD ["-XX:+UseContainerSupport", \
     "-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0", \
     "-Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom", \
     "-Dserver.port=8080", \
     "-jar", \
     "/app/app.jar"]

# Security hardening notes:
# - Secrets (DB passwords, API keys, tokens) must be injected via:
#   * Kubernetes Secrets mounted as environment variables
#   * Docker secrets
#   * External secret management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
# - Never bake secrets into the image
# - Run with: docker run --read-only --cap-drop=ALL --security-opt=no-new-privileges:true
# - In Kubernetes, use securityContext:
#   readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
#   runAsNonRoot: true
#   runAsUser: 1001
#   allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
#   capabilities:
#     drop: ["ALL"]
